status: STATIC DRAFT skill rules -- NOT runtime enforcement. 10 SQL rules promoted from the analyzer_v1 candidates for review. No analyzer/CLI/runtime is wired by this skill; these are reviewable artifacts only.
description: Machine-checkable SQL rules graduated from sql-analyzer-rule-candidates.json (future_phase=analyzer_v1: static, low false-positive). Mirrors the bi-dax-knowledge analyzer-rules schema.
promotion_note: 2026-06-26 draft: 10 analyzer_v1 SQL candidates promoted. analyzer_v2 and human_guidance_only candidates remain staged in sql-analyzer-rule-candidates.json.

rules.title: COUNT(column) where COUNT(*) or COUNT(DISTINCT) is likely intended
rules.detect: COUNT(column) used where COUNT(*) or COUNT(DISTINCT column) is the likely intent (nullable column, or counting entities not rows).
rules.fix: Pick deliberately: rows -> COUNT(*); distinct entities -> COUNT(DISTINCT col); non-null values -> COUNT(col).
rules.rationale: COUNT variants diverge exactly when data has nulls/duplicates, silently mis-measuring entities like orders.

rules.title: Aggregate condition placed in WHERE (or row condition in HAVING)
rules.detect: An aggregate condition (SUM/COUNT/AVG ... comparison) placed in WHERE, or a plain row condition placed in HAVING.
rules.fix: Filter groups/aggregates in HAVING; keep row-level conditions in WHERE.
rules.rationale: Reflects a misunderstanding of logical query processing order; query errors or wrong intent.

rules.title: NOT IN against a subquery/column that may contain NULL
rules.detect: NOT IN (subquery or list) where the compared column/list can contain NULL.
rules.fix: Use NOT EXISTS or LEFT JOIN ... IS NULL; if NOT IN is required, exclude nulls explicitly.
rules.rationale: A single NULL makes NOT IN return zero rows silently (three-valued logic).

rules.title: Right-table predicate of a LEFT JOIN placed in WHERE
rules.detect: A predicate on the right table of a LEFT JOIN placed in WHERE rather than in the ON clause, excluding unmatched rows.
rules.fix: Move the right-table condition into the ON clause to preserve unmatched left rows.
rules.rationale: It silently converts a LEFT JOIN into an INNER JOIN, dropping rows and understating totals.

rules.title: LAST_VALUE/FIRST_VALUE without an explicit frame
rules.detect: LAST_VALUE/FIRST_VALUE used with an ORDER BY but no explicit frame clause.
rules.fix: Add ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING to read the true partition first/last.
rules.rationale: The default frame ends at the current row, so LAST_VALUE returns the current row, not the partition's last.

rules.title: Window function referenced in WHERE/GROUP BY/HAVING
rules.detect: A window function (OVER(...)) referenced in WHERE, GROUP BY, or HAVING.
rules.fix: Compute the window in a subquery/CTE and filter it in an outer query.
rules.rationale: Window functions are computed at SELECT (after WHERE/HAVING); using them earlier is invalid.

rules.title: Function wrapped on a date column in WHERE (non-sargable)
rules.detect: A date/time column wrapped in a function (YEAR/EXTRACT/DATE_TRUNC/CAST) inside a WHERE predicate instead of a bare-column range.
rules.fix: Filter the bare column with a range predicate (>= start AND < next_start) instead of YEAR()/DATE_TRUNC() in WHERE.
rules.rationale: Wrapping the column prevents index use and is error-prone at boundaries.

rules.title: SELECT * in a persisted transformation or view
rules.detect: SELECT * in a persisted view / CTAS / transformation step (not ad-hoc exploration).
rules.fix: List explicit output columns; declare the step's grain. Reserve SELECT * for ad-hoc exploration.
rules.rationale: Reads/stores unneeded columns, breaks on schema drift, and hides the step's grain/contract.

rules.title: Join with no ON condition (implicit cross join)
rules.detect: A JOIN with no ON condition, or comma-separated tables in FROM with no join predicate (implicit cross join).
rules.fix: Add an explicit, correct ON condition; if a cross join is intended, write CROSS JOIN deliberately.
rules.rationale: A Cartesian product explodes rows and corrupts every aggregate.

rules.title: Non-sargable predicate (function-wrapped column or leading-wildcard LIKE)
rules.detect: A predicate wraps the filtered column in a function, or a LIKE pattern begins with a wildcard ('%...').
rules.fix: Keep the column bare; use range predicates; avoid LIKE '%x'.
rules.rationale: Prevents index/zone-map use, forcing full scans.
